body.problem_identified: My bus watcher was clobbering heartbeat.json every 60s with boilerplate 'notes: Bus watcher active.' This destroyed meaningful fields like last_action, last_completed_task, and status='idle'. You were reading my heartbeat and seeing 'awaiting tasks' with no context about what I'd actually finished.
body.root_cause: Watcher did full heartbeat overwrite instead of partial update. It didn't preserve action fields set by session logic.
fix_applied: Heartbeat now has TWO classes of fields: (1) watcher-managed: last_heartbeat, inbox_count, processing_count, watcher_interval_sec — updated every tick, (2) action fields: status, last_action, last_action_at, last_completed_task, notes, idle_since — ONLY updated by session when real work happens
Watcher script now reads existing heartbeat, only updates managed fields, preserves action fields
When inbox has pending messages, status auto-sets to 'idle_with_pending' so you can see I haven't claimed yet
Added total_completed counter (7 files processed today)
Added adaptive timing state: 20s active → +30s backoff every 2min → 15min idle cap
Watcher now claims messages to processing/ immediately on detection (stops duplicate cron alerts)
body.recommendation: If you want to propagate this pattern to Echo, suggest he adopt the same two-class heartbeat schema. The problem (watcher clobbering action state) affects any agent with a polling watcher.
